entities.label: CONSTRAINT

text: System shall be able to provide access to transaction history and available balance for account holders.
System will handle orders with multiple image types if the PACS system can only process each image separately.

entities.label: CONSTRAINT

text: The product shall allow a player to initiate a game by selecting another available player from the list of players.
Users will be able to Save their game if playing in single player mode.

entities.label: CONSTRAINT
entities.text: if playing in single player mode

text: Developer shall provide FABS groups that function under the FREC paradigm.
User shall be able to edit assets belonging to their department if authorized, at administrative, faculty, or university levels.

entities.label: CONSTRAINT
entities.text: if authorized, at administrative, faculty, or university levels

text: Developer shall provide FABS groups functioning under the FREC paradigm for consistency and efficiency.

entities.label: CONSTRAINT
entities.text: under the FREC paradigm for consistency and efficiency

text: System shall be able to display the most searched items on the title screen when the Encyclopedia app is launched.

entities.label: CONSTRAINT
entities.text: when the Encyclopedia app is launched

text: The system shall be able to calculate possible Chow/Pung/Kong/Mahjong moves within 5 seconds of receiving the latest discard information.
User details and data should be stored securely in a database provided by the server.
System shall be able to support a monitor with a minimum size of 14 inches and VGA resolution.
The system shall provide the ability to re-assign and route tasks from one user to another user.

metadata.id: CCHIT Certified 2011 Ambulatory EHR Criteria 20110517 - parsed.json_189

text: The system shall provide the ability to display information which has been inactivated or removed from the allergy and adverse reaction list.

metadata.id: CCHIT Certified 2011 Ambulatory EHR Criteria 20110517 - parsed.json_42

text: (2) A qualifying MA organization must provide CMS with the following for each MA EP or eligible hospital when reporting under either paragraph (b)(1) or (b)(3) of this section:

entities.label: CONSTRAINT
entities.text: under either paragraph (b)(1) or (b)(3) of this section

text: Only authorized user shall access the RFS system.
The system shall store medication information in discrete data fields.

metadata.id: CCHIT Certified 2011 Ambulatory EHR Criteria 20110517 - parsed.json_30

text: All Users will be able to view the next product by swiping left to right.
The solution should display additional inmate information during the order entry process.
The solution should provide alerts or reminders when departing from inmate-specific formulary.
The system shall provide end-users the ability to search for medications by generic or brand name.

metadata.id: CCHIT Certified 2011 Ambulatory EHR Criteria 20110517 - parsed.json_116

text: The system shall provide the ability to view previously generated reports by all users or by specific users.
The solution should provide dose range checking by disease state (for all routes) during the order entry process.
The product shall provide GUI based Monitoring Services.
The system shall provide the ability to capture other clinical data elements as discrete data.

metadata.id: CCHIT Certified 2011 Ambulatory EHR Criteria 20110517 - parsed.json_71

text: The system shall provide the ability for multiple users to interact concurrently with the EHR application.

metadata.id: CCHIT Certified 2011 Ambulatory EHR Criteria 20110517 - parsed.json_229

text: The system shall provide the ability to view the rationale for a drug interaction alert.

metadata.id: CCHIT Certified 2011 Ambulatory EHR Criteria 20110517 - parsed.json_159

text: The solution should support the use of the National Provider Identifier (NPI) in the EMR audit log to identify the individual provider or in situations when an NPI is not available for an individual a single unique internal provider identifier is assigned.

entities.label: CONSTRAINT
entities.text: when an NPI is not available for an individual a single unique internal provider identifier is assigned

text: Access control.
An LHCP can schedule an appointment with a patient.
Selecting multiple encounters will allow you to generate batch files for many claims at once.
The interfaced solutions should send notifications to the Pharmacy system regarding machine load or unload.
The solution should provide documentation on information classification matrix of all information elements accessed possibly and possibly processed by the solution.
The solution should provide a direct interface with drug wholesaler information systems.
This will allow the Disputes application to request adjustments to cardmember and merchantâs accounts.
The solution should provide the ability to create standard assessments that correspond to the problem list.
The HCP can edit the patient according to data format 6.4 with all initial values (except patient MID) defaulting to null possibly and possibly 0 as appropriate.

Data Recipient shall use appropriate safeguards to protect the IIHI from misuse or inappropriate disclosure and to prevent any use or disclosure of the IIHI other than as provided in this DUA or as otherwise required by law or regulation.
Capture Data and Documentation from External Clinical Sources
The solution should provide the ability to create order set templates.
The solution should provide the ability to update access privilege rules.
The solution should support batch order filling at user-defined time periods.
The solution should provide the ability to track overdue preventive services.
The solution should provide standing order functionality with unlimited number of Meds IV or LVP IVPB etc.
The solution should provide the ability to use registries or directories to retrieve links to relevant healthcare information regarding a patient.
The solution should provide EMR security administrators with the ability to grant authorizations to principals according to scope of practice organizational policy or jurisdictional law.
(b) Subject to Â§ 495.332 the State must submit a State Medicaid HIT Plan to CMS that includesâ
The solution should provide drug order status of reinstated.
The solution should provide drug order status of conditional.
An LHCP can view a list of their upcoming appointment requests.
The solution should track the status of the ordered action or item.
A Lab Technician can update the status of a lab procedure to received.
The solution should provide designation of natural or homeopathic on the formulary.
The solution should provide notification of overdue preventative services in the patient record.
The solution should display the timing route of administration and dose of all medications on the list.
The solution should provide the ability for an authorized user to group results into clinically logical sections.
The solution should provide normal ranges for data based on age and other parameters such as height weight ethnic background gestational age.
(2) The National Coordinator will notify the applicant's authorized representative of its satisfactory application and its successful achievement of ONC-ACB status.
(b) An ONC-ACB must provide the option for a Complete EHR to be certified solely to the applicable certification criteria adopted by the Secretary at subpart C of this part.
